name: kimi-bash-force-push-deny
doc: Kimi Code CLI wire-shape parity. A destructive `git push --force` via the Kimi `Bash` tool (PascalCase tool names, same casing as Claude Code — verified kimi.com/code/docs/en/kimi-code-cli/reference/tools.html) matches GIT_BLOCKED → the only documented Kimi decision, `hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision:"deny"` (camelCase output despite the snake_case input — a real Kimi inconsistency, respond.ts kimi case -> adapters/kimi/toKimiResponse).

steps.doc: Destructive git via Bash -> GIT_BLOCKED deny, rendered as Kimi's documented `hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision:"deny"` envelope (never Claude's ask, never a bare `{decision:...}`).

150-line Write (Claude-shaped file_path+content, unverified for real Kimi field names but the reasonable default given Kimi's Claude-cased tool names) > 100 -> SOLID file-size block, proving the file-size gate runs on the kimi edition too.
